summary: The generic validator: evaluate a Contract's decidable clauses over extracted Features, emitting a Diagnostic per violation at the clause's declared severity — replaces the heuristic Rule registry.

per.section: Decision: kill the heuristic rule registry

new.description: `validate(&Contract, &[Features]) -> Vec<Diagnostic>`: for each artifact's features, evaluate every clause as a decidable predicate and, on a false predicate, push a `check::Diagnostic` whose severity is the clause's declared `required`->Error / `advisory`->Warn and whose `rule` id is the clause key. No `if`-buried opinion: the engine reads only declared clauses. Reuses `check::{Diagnostic, Severity}` and `contract::{Contract, Clause, Predicate, Severity}`.

edit.description: add `pub mod engine;` (this entry's module line only)

tests.asserts: each primitive clause fires exactly on a violating feature set and stays silent on a conforming one; declared severity maps required->Error / advisory->Warn; an all-advisory run yields no Error

acceptance: Given a hand-built Contract + Features, `validate` reproduces the surviving decidable findings (name-format, name-matches-dir, lengths, forbidden_keys, body max_lines) with correct severities; `cargo test`/`clippy -D warnings` green.
notes: Primary import `contract::Contract` already shipped (CONTRACT-MODEL). blockedBy names SKILL-EXTRACTOR because it consumes `extract::Features`. Does NOT re-introduce the rejected heuristics — there is no clause for trigger/anti-trigger/third-person/prose-grep refs.

summary: The Anthropic skill contract template as data — the decidable best-practice clauses that survive the registry kill, adopted by reference, never hardcoded.

new.description: Declarative skill contract: `name`/`description` required + min_len 1; `name` allowed_chars `[a-z0-9-]`, max_len 64, deny `[anthropic, claude]`; `description` max_len 1024; body max_lines 500 (advisory); forbidden_keys `[globs, alwaysApply]` (Cursor keys Claude Code ignores); `name-matches-dir`. Every clause decidable and in-crate — no `pattern`/regex, no trigger/no-op judgment.
Load `contracts/skill.anthropic.toml` through `contract::Contract` and assert it parses and carries exactly the surviving decidable clauses at their declared severities.

tests.asserts: the shipped template deserializes into the model and declares the expected clause set (name format/length/deny, description length, body max_lines advisory, forbidden_keys, name-matches-dir)

acceptance: `contracts/skill.anthropic.toml` loads into the Contract model and encodes only the decidable surviving clauses; the dropped heuristics are absent; `cargo test` green.
notes: Data, not code (law 2). `contract::Contract` (CONTRACT-MODEL) already shipped, so this is unblocked. This is the std-lib type an author adopts; it is what CHECK-CUTOVER embeds as the built-in default.

summary: Wire `author check` to load the built-in skill contract and run the generic engine over extracted Features; add `--deny-advisories` to promote advisories to blocking.

edit.description: Check loads the workspace, extracts `Features` per skill, validates against the built-in skill contract (`include_str!("../contracts/skill.anthropic.toml")` parsed by `contract::Contract`), renders via `check::render`, and exits non-zero on any required-clause violation; add `--deny-advisories` so advisory violations also fail. Stop calling `rules::all_rules` (main.rs:57). While rewriting the dispatch, refresh the stale module doc it makes inaccurate: the header (main.rs:1-9) narrates the rule-registration step this entry removes and (with the `DEFAULT_WORKSPACE` comment, main.rs:4,20) cites the absent `spec/RELEASE-v0.1.md` — repoint both to the evergreen `specs/` corpus (`00-intent.md` evergreen-spec decision), the main.rs twin of LIBDOC-EVERGREEN's lib.rs scrub.

keep the clean/error-exit cases (name-format + name-matches-dir still fire as errors); add a `--deny-advisories` case where an advisory-only workspace exits non-zero with the flag and zero without it.

tests.asserts: import->check exits 0 on a clean skill, non-zero on an uppercase/mismatched name; `--deny-advisories` flips an advisory-only run to non-zero

acceptance: The gate now runs the declared contract, not the registry; default blocks on `required` only, `--deny-advisories` also blocks on advisory; `cargo test`/`clippy -D warnings` green.
notes: Also depends on SKILL-EXTRACTOR + SKILL-CONTRACT-TEMPLATE (ordered above). Embeds the bundled template as the default; the override/selection mechanism is the (contract-selection) fork, deferred. Leaves `rules.rs` in place (still public, no dead-code warning) for RETIRE-HEURISTICS to remove.

summary: Delete the rejected heuristic rule registry and its dead fixtures/tests; move the acceptance snapshot onto the contract-engine path.

per.section: Decision: kill the heuristic rule registry

edit.description: remove `pub mod rules;` (this entry's line only)
drive `engine::validate` against the built-in skill contract instead of `all_rules()`; regenerate the diagnostic-set snapshot to the reduced surviving-clause set (import surface snapshot unchanged)

tests.asserts: import->validate->re-import over the contract path reproduces the surviving diagnostics with no surface diff; the dropped heuristics no longer appear

acceptance: `src/rules.rs`, `tests/rules.rs`, and the five dropped-heuristic fixtures are gone; `lib.rs` has no `rules` module; acceptance runs on the engine; regenerated snapshot reflects the decidable clauses only; `cargo test`/`clippy -D warnings` green.
notes: Scope honesty: this removes shipped behavior (third-person, has-trigger, has-anti-trigger, prose-grep companion-refs, refs-one-level-deep) — mandated by the corpus, which names trigger + companion-refs as false-positive sources. The decidable members survive as template clauses, not code.

summary: Scrub the stale release-line citations from src/lib.rs's crate doc — it points at SPEC.md + spec/RELEASE-v0.1.md, files the evergreen corpus rejected and no longer present.

per.section: Decision: evergreen spec, not release lines

edit.description: rewrite only the `//!` crate-doc header: replace the `See SPEC.md ... spec/RELEASE-v0.1.md for the active ship target` sentence with a pointer to the evergreen `specs/` corpus (`specs/00-intent.md` north star, continuously reconciled). Touch the doc block only — leave the `pub mod`/`pub const` lines to the engine-chain entries.

acceptance: `src/lib.rs`'s crate doc no longer cites `SPEC.md` or `spec/RELEASE-v0.1.md` (both absent) and points at the evergreen `specs/` corpus; `cargo build`/`clippy -D warnings` green.
notes: Pure doc-comment reconciliation — code↔corpus drift, no behavior change. Disjoint region from the module-list edits the engine-chain entries make to lib.rs; low priority, ordered last.
